weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — first test wave. Peak block: comp-specific movements at competition standards, and your first fresh maxes of the season. Everything is fresh this week; warm up fully before any tested effort, give it one honest max, and keep the rest of the session light. Still capped at 3 sets — no extra rounds before a max.
testsLineHtml: Test week — wave 1. Max-effort competition tests this week: KB Box Squat, Dynamax OH Throw, Bench Press, Overhead Arm Hang, and Standing Broad Jump. Warm up, take your best shot, and tap your name below to log each result. The remaining events test next week.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 1 max-effort AMRAP · go for max reps in the comp time cap · full sit to box, full stand each rep
patterns.load: Your exact competition KB at competition box height
cues: Set a steady pace from rep one — start too fast and you will stall.
Every rep must touch the box and reach a full lockout to count.
Push hard through the time cap — this is your fresh max test.
Breathe in rhythm and keep your chest up even as you fatigue.

patterns.name: Light Goblet Squat Flush
patterns.rx: 2 × 12 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · easy and smooth, just keep the legs moving
patterns.load: A KB 2 sizes LIGHTER than competition, held at the chest
cues: Keep this light and easy — it is supportive work, not a test.
Move smoothly through a comfortable depth with no grinding.
Stop well short of fatigue; you should finish feeling fresh.
Stand tall and breathe easy between reps.

patterns.name: Bodyweight Box Sit-to-Stand
patterns.rx: 2 × 10 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · slow and controlled, no load
patterns.load: Bodyweight only — light movement prep
cues: Sit to the box and stand under control to grease the comp pattern.
Keep it easy and clean — no rush, no fatigue.
Reach your arms forward for balance as you stand if needed.
Stop the moment your legs feel even slightly taxed.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: TEST: 3 max-effort throws for distance · full rest (2–3 min) between each · record your best throw in ft + in
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men), exactly comp standard
cues: This is your test: take 3 all-out throws for maximum distance, exactly like comp day.
Set up fully, take one breath, then explode up and throw the ball as far back over your head as you can.
Rest 2–3 minutes between throws so each attempt is completely fresh — this is about your single best throw.
Measure and record your best of the three in feet and inches.

patterns.name: Light KB Hinge — Movement Prep
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · easy and supportive only
patterns.load: A light KB you can hinge for 8 easy reps with zero strain
cues: Push your hips back and slide the bell down your thighs, then stand tall and squeeze your glutes — keep it light and easy.
This is just warm-up and prep for the test; never grind these reps.
Keep your back flat and the movement smooth, grooving the hinge before you throw.
Stop well short of fatigue — the throw test is the priority today.

patterns.name: Band Pull-Apart
patterns.rx: 2 × 12 reps · rest 45 sec between sets · light shoulder prep
patterns.load: A light resistance band held at shoulder height
cues: Hold the band in front at shoulder height and pull it apart until it touches your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Keep it light and easy — this primes the upper back that controls the ball overhead.
Return slowly under control and keep your arms straight throughout.
Don't shrug your shoulders up toward your ears; keep them down and relaxed.

patterns.name: Comp Bench Max-Rep Test
patterns.rx: 1 max-effort set · as many reps as you can at comp weight, taken to or near failure · rest fully first
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: This is your competition test — warm up thoroughly, then do one all-out set of as many clean reps as you can at comp weight.
Have a spotter or set the rack safeties — only push to true failure if you're safely set up.
Press each rep to a full lockout and pause briefly on the chest. Log your final rep count — that's your new max-rep number.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~60% of your max-rep test · rest 90 sec between
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — hit that number cleanly on all 3 sets and stop there.
Lower the bar to a light touch on your chest and press back up with a steady, controlled tempo.
End the set the instant the bar stalls or your hips lift off the bench.

patterns.name: Light DB Press Flush
patterns.rx: 2 × 12 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · easy and smooth
patterns.load: Two light DBs that feel easy for 12 reps
cues: Keep this light and supportive — press two easy DBs for smooth reps to flush the chest after your test.
Move through a full range with no strain at all; this is recovery work, not a challenge.
Stop well short of fatigue. The goal is to feel loose, not worked.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 1 max-effort hang for time · then 1 easy practice hold · full rest before the max
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at COMPETITION STANDARD — single all-out max hang to failure, hang as long as you possibly can
cues: This is your test: hang as long as you can, exactly like comp day.
Pack the shoulders, crush the bar, and fight to stay on past where it burns.
Breathe slow and steady; do not panic-clench and gas your grip early.
Have someone spot you so you step down safely at the very end, and log the time.

patterns.name: Easy Scap Activation
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps · rest as needed
patterns.load: Bodyweight hanging from the bar — light and easy, nowhere near failure
cues: Hang and gently pull just your shoulder blades down a few inches, arms straight.
Keep it light and smooth — this is prep, not a hard set.
Stop well short of any grip fatigue; you are saving it for the test.
Move slowly and feel the lats switch on each rep.

patterns.name: Light Band Face Pull
patterns.rx: 2 × 12 reps · rest as needed
patterns.load: A light resistance band anchored at chest height — easy tension only
cues: Pull the band toward your face, leading with the elbows high and wide.
Squeeze the rear shoulders gently; keep the effort light and supportive.
This keeps the shoulders healthy without taxing your grip before the test.
Stop the moment it feels like work — today is about the max hang only.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 sec · rest 90 sec between · comp pace
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball
cues: Short comp-pace bursts at race speed — sharp and full-reach, not to failure.
Treat these like a dress rehearsal: clean reps, full overhead, hard slam.
Keep volume low this week so you stay fresh for testing.

patterns.name: Light D-Ball Speed Slam
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: A light D-ball, 6 lb (women) / 12 lb (men)
cues: With a light ball, slam each rep as fast as humanly possible to prime your speed.
Full reach overhead, then accelerate straight down — quality over quantity.
This keeps the nervous system fast without adding fatigue.

patterns.name: Bird Dog
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps each side · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: On hands and knees, reach the opposite arm and leg out long, then return under control.
Keep your hips level and your trunk braced — no rocking side to side.
Light supportive core work to keep your back happy into comp.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 60 sec of singles · rest 2 min between sets · easy, smooth, low effort
patterns.load: Your jump rope, at an easy supportive pace
cues: Keep this light and smooth — you're staying sharp, not testing today.
Hold an easy rhythm and stop each set still feeling fresh.
Land soft and quiet to protect your legs for the test weeks.

patterns.name: Bodyweight Calf Pulses
patterns.rx: 2 × 20 reps each leg · rest 60 sec between sets · light and easy
patterns.load: Bodyweight only, rail for light balance
cues: Do small, easy rises onto the ball of one foot to keep the calves primed.
Keep these gentle — no burn, just light movement.
Use the rail only for balance, not to push yourself up.

patterns.name: Easy Bounce Skips
patterns.rx: 2 × 30 sec relaxed singles · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Your jump rope, relaxed easy pace
cues: Skip at a relaxed, easy pace just to stay loose and coordinated.
Keep the bounce small and the effort low.
Stop if you feel any heaviness — freshness is the goal this week.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: Best of 3 max jumps for distance · full rest between attempts · record your best in feet and inches
patterns.load: Bodyweight · competition standard
cues: This is your test: take 3 all-out jumps for maximum distance, fully rested between each, and log your best.
Stand both feet at the line, swing the arms back, snap the hips, and jump as far as you can — then stick the landing.
Only count a jump where you land and hold both feet; a stumble or fall doesn't score. Record your best in feet and inches.

patterns.name: Easy Glute Bridge Hold
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 sec holds · rest 45 sec between holds · easy and supportive
patterns.load: Bodyweight, lying on your back with knees bent and feet flat
cues: Lift your hips into a gentle straight bridge and hold, squeezing the glutes lightly — keep this easy.
Brace your stomach so your low back stays flat; this is light support work, not a hard effort.
Lower under control between holds. Keep it relaxed so you stay fresh for your test jumps.

patterns.name: Gentle Line Hops
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 hops · rest 60 sec between sets · light and springy
patterns.load: Bodyweight, hopping side to side over a line on the floor
cues: Hop softly over a floor line with both feet, staying light on the balls of your feet — keep it easy.
Small, quiet, springy hops only; this is to stay loose around your test, not to tire you out.
Hold a wall or rail if you want balance. Stop early if anything feels heavy.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 400m · rest 3 min fully between · crisp near-race pace
patterns.load: Rower · about 1 sec/500m slower than goal split, two clean pieces
cues: Rehearse your race split on two clean pieces — sharp, not maxed.
Lock in your stroke sequence: legs, back, arms, and the same in reverse on the recovery.
Stay relaxed in the shoulders and let the legs carry the pace.

patterns.name: Light Face-Pull
patterns.rx: 2 × 12 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A light cable or band — easy weight, this is supportive only this week
cues: Pull the handles toward your forehead and spread your hands apart at the end.
Keep it light and smooth — this keeps the upper back fresh, it's not a hard set.
Squeeze the shoulder blades, then return slowly under control.

patterns.name: Easy Bike Flush
patterns.rx: 1 × 8 min easy · light, conversational spin
patterns.load: Stationary bike · very easy pace, well below any working effort
cues: Spin easy to keep the legs moving without adding fatigue this peak week.
Keep it conversational the whole time — no hard efforts.
Sit comfortable and relaxed; this is recovery, not training.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 150-yd shuttle (3 × 50 yd) · rest 4 min · crisp race-pace turns
patterns.load: Bodyweight · run at comp race pace, submaximal and sharp
cues: Run two clean sets of three 50s at race pace to stay sharp without draining yourself.
Decelerate, plant, and re-accelerate exactly as you will on comp day — smooth and confident.
This is supportive this week; keep it light and let the tested events take the effort.

patterns.name: Tempo Stride 60s
patterns.rx: 3 × 60 yards · relaxed ~70% · rest as needed
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Glide through 60 yards at an easy three-quarter effort, staying smooth and tall.
Keep it relaxed and aerobic — this flushes the legs without adding fatigue.
Walk back fully; the goal is freshness, not a hard effort.

patterns.name: Ankle Hop Primer
patterns.rx: 2 × 15 small hops · springy and light · rest 60 sec
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Hop lightly off the balls of your feet, barely bending the knees, staying quick and elastic.
Keep contacts short and snappy — this primes your tendons without taxing your legs.
Stay light and easy; this is supportive prep, not a workout.

events.tag: Peak + Taper · comp rehearsal (sled, not tested)

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 yards · rest 3 min between · comp-pace rehearsal (not timed, never a test)
patterns.load: Competition load: 2 × 25 lb plates (women) / 2 × 45 lb plates (men)
cues: Rehearse the full 50 yards at comp pace and comp weight — sharp but not maxed, and remember sled is never a timed test.
Lock in your race setup: body at 45°, arms locked, pushing through your trunk with long steps and toe drive.
Rest fully between pushes and keep this supportive — save your fresh max effort for the tested events this week.

patterns.name: Tempo Goblet Squat
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between · light and easy
patterns.load: A light kettlebell or dumbbell at your chest, easy enough to keep tension off the joints
cues: Squat down slow and stand up smooth with a light weight to keep your legs primed without adding fatigue.
Stay tall through your chest and control the tempo — this is supportive work, not a hard set.
Keep it easy and stop well short of any strain; freshness is the goal this week.

patterns.name: Glute Bridge March
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps per side · rest 60 sec between · light activation
patterns.load: Bodyweight only, lying on your back with knees bent
cues: Bridge your hips up and slowly lift one knee at a time to wake up the glutes that drive your push.
Keep your hips level and square the whole time — don't let one side dip as you march.
Move slow and easy; this is light prep to stay sharp, not a burner.
